# fix(kaizen-observability): JsonlSink path resolve + O_NOFOLLOW symlink refusal (H2)

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `239e0c9749bc` — fix(kaizen-observability): JsonlSink path resolve + O_NOFOLLOW symlink refusal (H2)

**Body**:

Security-reviewer flagged that JsonlSink used Path(path) verbatim
without resolving traversal or applying O_NOFOLLOW. Attacker-planted
symlink at the trace destination silently redirected the trace stream.

Fix:
  * __init__ resolves the path via expanduser().resolve(strict=False)
    so .. segments normalize at construction (audit trail shows the
    actual destination, not the caller's unnormalized input)
  * __call__ opens via os.open(str(path), O_WRONLY|O_CREAT|O_APPEND|
    O_NOFOLLOW, 0o600) on POSIX — symlink at destination raises
    OSError (ELOOP) instead of being followed
  * file-mode bits are 0o600 (owner-only r/w) — trace streams may
    contain classified payload hashes
  * mode validation rejects anything but 'a' or 'w' (closes 'r'/'a+'
    misuse path)
  * docstring documents that callers MUST pre-validate tenant-derived
    paths against an allowlist — the sink resolves symlinks but does
    not reject .. traversal above an allowlist root (policy vs mechanism)
  * Windows fallback uses Path.open (O_NOFOLLOW is POSIX-specific)

4 regression tests at tests/regression/test_jsonl_sink_path_safety.py:
  * path_resolved_at_construction_normalizes_dotdot
  * jsonl_sink_refuses_symlink_target (POSIX)
  * jsonl_sink_writes_fresh_file_end_to_end (+ 0o600 mode check)
  * jsonl_sink_rejects_invalid_mode

PR#6 of issue #567 — security-reviewer feedback on PR#587 (H2).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
